# UC-22.14.2 · Audit Record Content Completeness for Privileged Actions

## Description

Validates that privileged account activity records include who, what, when, where, and outcome fields required for AU-3 content of audit records.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes sec and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Security. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize Windows events into CIM Authentication fields via props/transforms; (2) tune extractions for workgroup vs domain naming; (3) alert when `incomplete>0` for Tier0 hosts; (4) pair with AD schema docs for field mapping evidence. Indexes required: `index=sec`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security` OR CIM `Authentication` — EventCode 4672, 4728, 4732, 4756, etc..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize Windows events into CIM Authentication fields via props/transforms; (2) tune extractions for workgroup vs domain naming; (3) alert when `incomplete>0` for Tier0 hosts; (4) pair with AD schema docs for field mapping evidence.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=sec sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security earliest=-24h EventCode IN (4672,4728,4732,4756,4767,4720,4722,4725)
| eval missing_user=if(isnull(user) OR user="",1,0)
| eval missing_dest=if(isnull(dest) OR dest="",1,0)
| eval missing_outcome=if(isnull(signature) OR signature="",1,0)
| eval au3_gap=missing_user+missing_dest+missing_outcome
| stats count as events, sum(au3_gap) as incomplete by EventCode, host
| where incomplete>0
```

Understanding this SPL

**Audit Record Content Completeness for Privileged Actions (AU-3)** — Validates that privileged account activity records include who, what, when, where, and outcome fields required for AU-3 content of audit records.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` OR CIM `Authentication` — EventCode 4672, 4728, 4732, 4756, etc. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: sec; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Security. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=sec, sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **missing_user** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **missing_dest** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **missing_outcome** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **au3_gap** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by EventCode, host** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where incomplete>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Audit Record Content Completeness for Privileged Actions (AU-3)** — Validates that privileged account activity records include who, what, when, where, and outcome fields required for AU-3 content of audit records.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` OR CIM `Authentication` — EventCode 4672, 4728, 4732, 4756, etc. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (EventCode, host, incomplete counts), Bar chart (gaps by host), Single value (incomplete rate)

## SPL

```spl
index=sec sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security earliest=-24h EventCode IN (4672,4728,4732,4756,4767,4720,4722,4725)
| eval missing_user=if(isnull(user) OR user="",1,0)
| eval missing_dest=if(isnull(dest) OR dest="",1,0)
| eval missing_outcome=if(isnull(signature) OR signature="",1,0)
| eval au3_gap=missing_user+missing_dest+missing_outcome
| stats count as events, sum(au3_gap) as incomplete by EventCode, host
| where incomplete>0
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (EventCode, host, incomplete counts), Bar chart (gaps by host), Single value (incomplete rate)
